mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in new home sales and rising residential construction spending indicates strengthening household balance sheets and durable goods demand, which directly boosts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both increase, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months where INDPRO drops >1.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to anticipate tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases >0.4%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into immediate positive market repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 increases, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent weakness in industrial production signals structural supply-side degradation and reduced capacity utilization, which suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of months where INDPRO trends downward, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a positive information coefficient in walk-forward validation.
